examples.title: Level-Order Traversal
examples.description: Breadth-First Search traversal of a binary tree using a queue
examples.code:
FUNCTION levelOrder(root)
 IF root = NULL THEN
 RETURN
 ENDIF
 
 CREATE queue Q
 ENQUEUE(Q, root)
 
 WHILE Q is not empty DO
 node = DEQUEUE(Q)
 VISIT(node)
 
 IF node.left != NULL THEN
 ENQUEUE(Q, node.left)
 ENDIF
 
 IF node.right != NULL THEN
 ENQUEUE(Q, node.right)
 ENDIF
 ENDWHILE
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Uses a queue to process nodes level by level. Start with root, visit it, add children to queue. Continue until all nodes are processed. This implements Breadth-First Search (BFS).
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w is maximum width of tree

examples.title: Deep Copy of Binary Tree
examples.description: Recursive algorithm to create an independent copy of a binary tree
examples.code:
FUNCTION deepCopy(node)
 IF node = NULL THEN
 RETURN NULL
 ENDIF
 
 // Create new node with copied data
 newNode = ALLOCATE_NEW_NODE()
 newNode.data = node.data
 
 // Recursively copy subtrees
 newNode.left = deepCopy(node.left)
 newNode.right = deepCopy(node.right)
 
 RETURN newNode
END FUNCTION

// To use:
// newTreeRoot = deepCopy(originalRoot)
examples.explanation: Uses pre-order traversal to create new nodes. For each node in original, allocate new memory and copy the data. Recursively copy left and right subtrees. Returns root of the independent copy.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) for recursion stack + O(n) for new tree storage

examples.title: Insert Node in Complete Binary Tree
examples.description: Insert a new node maintaining complete binary tree property
examples.code:
FUNCTION insertNode(root, value)
 newNode = CREATE_NODE(value)
 
 IF root = NULL THEN
 RETURN newNode
 ENDIF
 
 // Use queue to find first available position
 CREATE queue Q
 ENQUEUE(Q, root)
 
 WHILE Q is not empty DO
 temp = DEQUEUE(Q)
 
 IF temp.left = NULL THEN
 temp.left = newNode
 BREAK
 ELSE
 ENQUEUE(Q, temp.left)
 ENDIF
 
 IF temp.right = NULL THEN
 temp.right = newNode
 BREAK
 ELSE
 ENQUEUE(Q, temp.right)
 ENDIF
 ENDWHILE
 
 RETURN root
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Finds the first node with an empty child slot using level-order traversal. Inserts new node as left child if left is empty, otherwise as right child. Maintains complete tree property.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w is maximum width
